markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Expert Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3363) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 121

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Prerequisites**
[Basic Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3359)

</column>

---

You learn more powerful spells to enchant and deceive. You learn a 4th-level occult spell. At 12th level, you learn a 5th-level spell, and at 14th level, you learn a 6th-level spell. Each of these spells must come from either the [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) or [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) school. You Cast these Spells as occult innate spells. You become an expert with spell attack rolls and spell DCs for occult spells.

<title level="2">Expert Captivator Spellcasting leads to...</title>
[Master Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3366)
name: Expert Captivator Spellcasting

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reactive Charm](/Feats.aspx?ID=3364) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 121

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Captivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3358)

**Trigger**
You are the target of a spell or attack.

</column>

---

You're so charming and distracting that when pressed, you can cast a simple enchantment in time to foil an attacker. You cast any of your 1st-level [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) spells that normally take two actions or fewer to cast. This takes effect before the triggering spell or attack, and if the attacker is affected by your spell, it can make different decisions based on the effects (for instance, if you [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) the attacker and it becomes [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18) to you, it can choose a different target than you for its attack or spell). The attacking creature must be in range of your chosen spell, and if your spell requires targets, the attacking creature must be a valid target. Your spell affects only the attacking creature, even if it would normally affect more targets or an area.
name: Reactive Charm

[Effortless Captivation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3365) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
</traits>

You maintain your innate [enchantments](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) and [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) with hardly a thought. You immediately gain the effects of the Sustain a Spell action, which you can use only to extend the duration of an enchantment or illusion spell.

Each of these spells must come from either the [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) or [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) school. You cast these spells as occult innate spells. markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Look Again](/Feats.aspx?ID=3367) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 121

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Captivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3358)

</column>

---

If your [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) or [enchantments](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) fail to ensnare the minds of others, you can cast them again. If your next action is to cast an innate enchantment or illusion spell and it fails to affect any targets, you do not lose the spell. You must use the same number of actions to recast the same spell on your following turn; if you can't, or choose not to, you waste that number of actions with no effect. After this second casting, you lose the spell whether it affected any targets or not. Look Again works only with spells that require targets and doesn't do anything if you cast a targeted spell but didn't select any targets.
name: Look Again

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 122

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
Able to cast spells; Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

Your experience with magic and its traditions lets you specialize in the casting of certain spells, customizing familiar spells to create novel effects. Whenever you gain a feat from this archetype, you either learn to modify the effects of a single spell, or you change one of the modifications from a previous feat. Each time you Cast the Spell corresponding to the feat you chose, you decide whether to cast its normal or modified version. You can only apply one modification to a spell at a time, even if you know more than one modification for that spell. Besides modifications mentioned in the feat, the spell functions as normal.

 Choose up to two 4th-level spell trickster archetype feats for which you meet the spell-casting prerequisite. You gain those feats, ignoring their level prerequisite.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the spell trickster archetype. The two feats you gain from taking the dedication don’t count toward this total.

<title level="2">Spell Trickster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Agile Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=3369), [Animate Net](/Feats.aspx?ID=3376), [Barrier Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=3370), [Confounding Image](/Feats.aspx?ID=3377), [Directed Poison](/Feats.aspx?ID=3393), [Disk Rider](/Feats.aspx?ID=3378), [Drenching Mist](/Feats.aspx?ID=3379), [Forceful Push](/Feats.aspx?ID=3371), [Lingering Flames](/Feats.aspx?ID=3385), [Obscured Terrain](/Feats.aspx?ID=3380), [Scattered Fire](/Feats.aspx?ID=3386), [Shining Arms](/Feats.aspx?ID=3372), [Siphoning Touch](/Feats.aspx?ID=3387), [Smoldering Explosion](/Feats.aspx?ID=3388), [Steal Vitality](/Feats.aspx?ID=3389), [Summon Ensemble](/Feats.aspx?ID=3373), [Surrounding Flames](/Feats.aspx?ID=3390), [Toppling Tentacles](/Feats.aspx?ID=3391), [Tracing Sigil](/Feats.aspx?ID=3374), [Uneasy Rest](/Feats.aspx?ID=3381), [Volatile Grease](/Feats.aspx?ID=3382), [Wild Lights](/Feats.aspx?ID=3375)
name: Spell Trickster Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Able to cast spells; Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Agile Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=3369) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 122

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_mage hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=177)

</column>

---

When you cast _mage hand_, you can modify its target to be a set of [thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58). When you Cast the Spell in this way, the tools move up to 20 feet towards a device or lock. After you Sustain the Spell on future turns, you can use the tools to attempt [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) or [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) within the spell's range, but you take a –2 penalty to your Thievery check. If you critically fail, the spell ends and you can't use this modification again for 24 hours.

<title level="2">Agile Hand leads to...</title>
[Larcenous Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=3384)
name: Agile Hand
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_mage hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=177)

<title

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Barrier Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=3370) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 122

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)

</column>

---

When you cast _shield_, you can modify the spell to create a solid barrier you can use for cover, but not for blocking. You can't use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction when the spell is modified in this way, but you (and only you) can spend an action to [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90) to gain standard cover from it.
name: Barrier Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Forceful Push](/Feats.aspx?ID=3371) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=15" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 122

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_mage hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=177)

</column>

---

When you cast _mage hand_, you can modify its target to be 1 creature. If you do, replace its standard effects with the following: You push your foe telekinetically. Make a spell attack roll against the target's Fortitude DC. On a success, you [push](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the target 5 feet away from you. On a critical success, you push the target 10 feet away from you.
name: Forceful Push
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_mage hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=177)

<title

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shining Arms](/Feats.aspx?ID=3372) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)

</column>

---

When you cast _light_, you can modify its target to be 1 melee weapon, either unattended or possessed by you or a willing ally, and modify its duration to be 1 minute. If you do, add the following to its effects: When a creature wielding the weapon critically hits a foe, you can Dismiss the Spell as a reaction, causing the foe to be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round. After you use this reaction, you can't use this modification again for 10 minutes.
name: Shining Arms

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Summon Ensemble](/Feats.aspx?ID=3373) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 123

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_summon instrument_](/Spells.aspx?ID=721)

</column>

---

When you cast _summon instrument_, you can modify its duration to be sustained, up to 1 minute. When you do, you summon a number of instruments equal to your spellcasting ability modifier that hover around you and play of their own accord. Once per turn when you Sustain the Spell, choose an opponent within 30 feet who can hear your performance. The target must attempt a Will save against the spell's DC; on a failure, it becomes distracted by your performance and becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round. The target is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
name: Summon Ensemble
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_summon instrument_](/Spells.aspx?ID=721)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tracing Sigil](/Feats.aspx?ID=3374) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 123

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=286)

</column>

---

When you cast _sigil_, you can modify the spell to add the following to its standard effects: The target leaves a magical trail that you and others can try to follow. You and other creatures can attempt to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) the target, substituting an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check (whichever matches the magical tradition of your sigil spell) for the [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check. Much like Tracking with Survival, this must take place somewhere the target has been and follow the trail; it doesn't allow anyone to find the target from a distance. You can only have a single target marked with a modified sigil in this way. If you use this ability again on a second target, the _sigil_ spell on the first target ends, and your mark fades.

<title level="2">Tracing Sigil leads to...</title>
[Beacon Mark](/Feats.aspx?ID=3383)
name: Tracing Sigil

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

<summary>

</summary>
summary_markdown: When you cast _sigil_, you can modify the spell to allow you to track the target.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wild Lights](/Feats.aspx?ID=3375) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 123

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_dancing lights_](/Spells.aspx?ID=58)

</column>

---

When you cast _dancing lights_, you can modify its duration to be 1 minute and modify its standard effects to create a single floating light in the shape of a Tiny creature, instead of up to four floating lights. The creature hovers over your head unless you spend a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait to direct the light to move up to 30 feet, in which case it remains there until you direct it again. If you direct it back to your head, it hovers there and follows you again until you direct it elsewhere. If the light ever moves beyond 120 feet of you, it winks out immediately.
name: Wild Lights
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_dancing lights_](/Spells.aspx?ID=58)

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 123

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_animate rope_](/Spells.aspx?ID=667)

</column>

---

When you cast _animate rope_, you can modify its target to be 1 [net](/Equipment.aspx?ID=681). If you do, replace the spell's standard effects with the following: You cause a net to animate and throw itself at a Medium or smaller creature within 20 feet of the net. Attempt a spell attack roll against the target's Reflex DC. On a success, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79); on a critical success, it's also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it Escapes. The Escape DC is equal to your spell DC as long as the spell lasts. Once the spell ends, the Escape DC returns to the normal DC for a net (usually 16), and a creature adjacent to the target can Interact to remove the net.
name: Animate Net
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_animate rope_](/Spells.aspx?ID=667)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Confounding Image](/Feats.aspx?ID=3377) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 123

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197)

</column>

---

When you cast _mirror image_, you can modify its duration to be sustained up to 1 minute and modify its range to 30 feet. If you do, replace the spell's standard effects with the following: You create an illusory duplicate of yourself that appears in an unoccupied square within range. The image takes up space and has a reach of 5 feet, and your allies can flank with the image. It doesn't have any other attributes a creature would normally have other than an AC equal to your spell DC and saving throw modifiers equal to your spell DC – 10. If the image is hit by an attack or fails a save, or if you ever move more than 30 feet from it, the spell ends. Creatures can move through its space without hindrance. When you Sustain the Spell, you can move the image to an unoccupied square within range.
name: Confounding Image
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197)

[Disk Rider](/Feats.aspx?ID=3378) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_floating disk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=124)

</column>

---

When you cast _floating disk_, you can modify its duration to be sustained, up to 10 minutes. If you do, add the following heightened entry to its effects.

**Heightened (3rd)** The disk can hold up to 10 Bulk. The spell no longer ends if you ride atop the disk (though it does if any other creature rides atop it, including if the creature is riding on you). If you are riding the disk, it changes its capacity to hold you, plus additional Bulk equal to the maximum you could carry without being [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12). If you ride atop the disk, you can direct it to move along the ground up to 30 feet or your Speed, whichever is slower, each time you Sustain the Spell. While you ride atop it, the disk can move over liquids as long as it ends its movement on solid ground. If it ends its movement on a surface that can't support it, the spell ends.
name: Disk Rider
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_floating disk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=124)

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 124 **Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126) **Prerequisites** [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) --- When you cast _grease_, you can modify its target to be 1 creature. If you do, replace the spell's standard effects with the following: You splash the target with combustible grease. The target must attempt a Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is splattered with grease and gains weakness 2 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) until the end of your next turn. The target or an adjacent creature can rub off the combustible grease with an Interact action, ending the effect.
**Failure** As success, except the weakness to fire lasts for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (+2)** The weakness increases by 1.
name: Volatile Grease

</column>

---

When you cast _sigil_, you can modify the spell to add the following heightened entry.

 **Heightened (4th)** You attune yourself to the marked target. While the spell lasts, you can spend a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), and [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) traits, to attempt to locate the target, learning the direction to the target as long as you are within 1 mile of it. If the target is a creature or a creature is in possession of the target object, the creature can attempt a Will saving throw against your spell DC. If it succeeds, your attempt to locate the target fails and you can't attempt this again for 1 day. You can only have a single target marked with a _sigil_ modified in this way. If you Cast this Spell again on a second target, the _sigil_ spell on the first target ends, and your mark fades.
name: Beacon Mark

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Agile Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=3369); [Pickpocket](/Feats.aspx?ID=818)

</column>

---

When you cast [_mage hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), you can remove its duration, modify its range to 20 feet, and modify its target to be an attended object within the spell's Bulk limit that wouldn't be time-consuming to remove. Add the following to the spell's standard effects: If the creature attending the object is willing to have you take the item, _mage hand_ carries the item to you. If the creature is unwilling, you must attempt to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) the target object with a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check. The usual restrictions on attempts to Steal an object apply, including the restrictions that you can't steal objects that would be extremely noticeable or time-consuming to remove (like worn shoes or armor or actively wielded objects) and you can't Steal from a creature in combat or otherwise on guard. On a successful Steal check, the _mage hand_ carries the item to you, and on a critical failure, you can't use this modification again for 1 hour. If you're a master in Thievery, you can attempt to Steal from a creature in combat or otherwise on guard, though if you do so, the spell's casting time increases by 1 action.
name: Larcenous Hand

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Toppling Tentacles](/Feats.aspx?ID=3391) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 125

**Archetype** [Spell Trickster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=126)

**Prerequisites**
[Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_black tentacles_](/Spells.aspx?ID=23)

</column>

---

When you cast _black tentacles_, replace the spell's standard effects with the following: Tentacles slither along the ground in the area, attempting to hinder anyone within. Sticky black tentacles attempt to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) each creature in the area. Attempt spell attack rolls against the Reflex DCs of each affected creature. Any creature you succeed against is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and takes 3d6 bludgeoning damage. Whenever a creature ends its turn in the area, the tentacles attempt to Trip that creature if it isn't already prone, and they deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage to any creature already prone. Creatures treat the spell's area as difficult terrain.
name: Toppling Tentacles
prerequisite_markdown: [Spell Trickster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3368); ability to cast [_black tentacles_](/Spells.aspx?ID=23)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wrestler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3394) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 126

**Archetype** [Wrestler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=127)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3); trained in [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks, and unarmored defense

</column>

---

Your training in the wrestling arts has made you particularly adept at moving, striking, and grappling while unencumbered. You become an expert in Athletics and gain the [Titan Wrestler](/Feats.aspx?ID=854) skill feat. You don't take the –2 circumstance penalty for making a lethal attack with your [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) unarmed attacks. In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC when resisting an opponent's attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) you or [Swallow you Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35).

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the wrestler archetype.

<title level="2">Wrestler Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aerial Piledriver](/Feats.aspx?ID=3402), [Clinch Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=3398), [Disengaging Twist](/Feats.aspx?ID=3395), [Elbow Breaker](/Feats.aspx?ID=3396), [Form Lock (Wrestler)](/Feats.aspx?ID=3405), [Inescapable Grasp](/Feats.aspx?ID=3404), [Running Tackle](/Feats.aspx?ID=3399), [Spinebreaker](/Feats.aspx?ID=3403), [Strangle](/Feats.aspx?ID=3400), [Submission Hold](/Feats.aspx?ID=3401), [Suplex](/Feats.aspx?ID=3397)
name: Wrestler Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3); trained in [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks, and unarmored defense

Make an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee Strike against the creature you have grabbed or restrained; on a success, the target lands [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), and on a critical success, the target lands prone and takes an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage. [Strangle](/Feats.aspx?ID=3400) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 127

[Wrestler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3394)

**Requirements**
You have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33).

</column>

---

You twist and squeeze the breath out of your foe. Make an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee Strike against the creature you have grabbed or restrained. On a success, you gain a circumstance bonus to damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice and the target can barely speak until the start of your next turn or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), whichever comes first. While it can barely speak, the target can't vocalize above a hoarse whisper, and it must succeed at a DC 10 flat check or lose any action that requires speech. For an action requiring speech that is also a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action, like Casting a Spell with somatic and verbal components, the target just rolls a single DC 10 flat check, rather than an additional DC 5 flat check for being grabbed.
name: Strangle

>
[Inescapable Grasp](/Feats.aspx?ID=3404) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Wrestler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3394)

</column>

---

Your grasp has a supernatural quality to it, preventing your foes from easily escaping—with or without magical assistance. If a creature you have grabbed attempts to use a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) spell or effect, it must succeed at a DC 15 flat check or the spell fails. If a creature you have grabbed attempts to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) while under the effect of [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), it must succeed at a DC 15 flat check or be forced to roll the Escape attempt normally, rather than automatically succeeding.
name: Inescapable Grasp

[Form Lock (Wrestler)](/Feats.aspx?ID=3405) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=15" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 127

**Archetypes** [Wrestler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=127), [Wrestler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=270) (Level 14)

**Prerequisites**
[Wrestler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3394)

**Requirements**
You have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33).

</column>

---

Your ability to manipulate your enemy's body is potent enough that you can tear and break apart alternate forms. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effect currently affecting the creature you have grabbed or restrained. If the target is somehow under the effect of multiple polymorph effects, you can choose which one to attempt to counteract; the GM chooses randomly if the separate effects aren't obvious. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 day.
name: Form Lock (Wrestler)
search_markdown:

    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cloud Walk](/Feats.aspx?ID=3406) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Dragon Disciple](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=56)

**Prerequisites**
[Dragon Disciple Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1940)

</column>

---

Your growing link to cloud dragons has granted you the ability to walk on clouds. Once per hour, you can cast [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) on yourself as an innate arcane spell, but only to walk on clouds or fog.

<title level="2">Bloodsalt Dragon Disciple Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 79

The following archetype feats are for the dragon disciple archetype. You must take the Dragon Disciple Dedication feat before selecting any of these feats, and you must select cloud dragon (which is associated with the electricity energy type) as the type of dragon you've studied. The feats below are dragon disciple archetype feats. By studying the ruins (see the AP for full details), you gain access to the dragon disciple archetype and to these feats

 If you have the [draconic sorcerer](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5) bloodline, you must choose cloud dragon as your dragon type. This choice affects the [_dragon breath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=499) and [_dragon claws_](/Spells.aspx?ID=500) bloodline spells and several of the feats below.

**Related Feats**: [Deflecting Cloud](/Feats.aspx?ID=3408), [Mighty Wings](/Feats.aspx?ID=3409), [Shape of the Cloud Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=3407)
name: Cloud Walk

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Dragon Disciple](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=56)

**Frequency**
once every 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Dragon Disciple Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1940); [_dragon wings_](/Spells.aspx?ID=501) sorcerer bloodline spell

**Trigger**
You are the target of a ranged attack.

**Requirements**
You are aware of the attack and currently have _dragon wings_ active.

</column>

---

You've mastered a defensive technique unique to the ancient dragons of Bloodsalt: beating your wings to create a billowing cloud of mist. You become momentarily [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from the triggering attack if vision is the attacker's only precise sense. Normally this means the attacker must succeed at a DC 11 flat check to target you. You also gain a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

<title level="2">Bloodsalt Dragon Disciple Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 79

You must take the Dragon Disciple Dedication feat before selecting any of these feats, and you must select cloud dragon (which is associated with the electricity energy type) as the type of dragon you've studied. The feats below are dragon disciple archetype feats. By studying the ruins (see the AP for full details), you gain access to the dragon disciple archetype and to these feats

 If you have the [draconic sorcerer](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5) bloodline, you must choose cloud dragon as your dragon type. This choice affects the [_dragon breath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=499) and [_dragon claws_](/Spells.aspx?ID=500) bloodline spells and several of the feats below.

**Related Feats**: [Cloud Walk](/Feats.aspx?ID=3406), [Mighty Wings](/Feats.aspx?ID=3409), [Shape of the Cloud Dragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=3407)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Blessing of the Sun Gods](/Feats.aspx?ID=3410) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Bright Lion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=129)

**Prerequisites**
[Bright Lion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2172)

</column>

---

The [Old Sun Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=19) share their power with you. Select the [ambition](/Domains.aspx?ID=1), [cities](/Domains.aspx?ID=3), [darkness](/Domains.aspx?ID=6), [dreams](/Domains.aspx?ID=9), [family](/Domains.aspx?ID=11), [fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=13), [freedom](/Domains.aspx?ID=14), [healing](/Domains.aspx?ID=15), [moon](/Domains.aspx?ID=21), [passion](/Domains.aspx?ID=25), or [sun](/Domains.aspx?ID=29) domain. You gain an initial domain spell for that domain. If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can regain using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity to pray to the Old Sun Gods; if you already have a focus pool, increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. Your Bright Lion focus spells are divine spells; you become trained in divine spell attacks and spell DCs, using Charisma as your spellcasting ability score.

**Special** You can select this feat multiple times, selecting a different domain each time and gaining its domain spell.

<title level="2">Blessing of the Sun Gods leads to...</title>
[Greater Sun Blessing](/Feats.aspx?ID=3413)
name: Blessing of the Sun Gods

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pactbound Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3414) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 10

**Archetype** [Pactbound Initiate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=167)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10); [Erutaki](/Languages.aspx?ID=54) or [Giant](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) language, must have earned the trust of a saumen kar who initiates you into the archetype

</column>

---

You befriended one of the last saumen kar and were infused with a fraction of their power, granting you the potential to become a guardian of their ancient pact. You gain [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) resistance equal to half your level. You can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) while it's snowing even lightly, even if it wouldn't be thick enough to normally grant [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). Finally, all saumen kar can sense the mark of their pact upon you, and their attitude toward you is typically one step better than it would be otherwise ([hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) to [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=40), unfriendly to [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25), etc.) Most saumen kar are unfriendly to outsiders; this would make them indifferent to you.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the pactbound initiate archetype.

<title level="2">Pactbound Dedication leads to...</title>
[Crown of the Saumen Kar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3416), [Frostbite Runes](/Feats.aspx?ID=3417), [Ice Crafter](/Feats.aspx?ID=3415)
name: Pactbound Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10); [Erutaki](/Languages.aspx?ID=54) or [Giant](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) language, must have earned the trust of a saumen kar who initiates you into the archetype

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Pactbound Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3414)

</column>

---

The ancient ice-crafting techniques of the saumen kar awaken within you. You become trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), or become an expert if you were already trained. You gain the [Magical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=812) skill feat. You can craft permanent items out of ice instead of metal or wood; these items are a translucent crystal-blue in color, resemble icicles and glacier ice, and have the same statistics and properties as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272), including the price and the ability to deal additional damage to certain kinds of creatures.

 Any creature who wields or wears a piece of ice-crafted equipment takes 1 cold damage each round from contact with the primal cold of the crafted ice.
name: Ice Crafter

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crown of the Saumen Kar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3416) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 10

**Archetype** [Pactbound Initiate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=167)

**Prerequisites**
[Pactbound Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3414)

</column>

---

Your body begins to evolve and adapt, taking the first steps towards blending your very self with the ancient curse of the saumen kar. You become larger and bulkier, pushing the very edge of height and weight for your size. You gain [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) as an imprecise sense with a range of 30 feet and gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks made to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) or [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) in areas of forest and snow. You sprout a pair of horns from your forehead, gaining a horns [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack that deals 1d8 bludgeoning damage. Your horns are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193) and [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196) traits.
name: Crown of the Saumen Kar

**Prerequisites**
[Pactbound Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3414)

</additional-info>

---

<summary>
Your body begins to evolve and adapt, taking the first steps towards blending your very self with the ancient curse of the saumen kar.
</summary>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Frostbite Runes](/Feats.aspx?ID=3417) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 11

**Archetype** [Pactbound Initiate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=167)

**Prerequisites**
[Pactbound Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3414)

</column>

---

Thick tattoos, each one a rune fragment from the saumen kar's long-lost language spelling out their pact with their equally lost deity, cover your body and stretch across your limbs, torso, and face. You gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) and [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) spells and effects. You can sense the presence of evil [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) as a pervasive taste of oily corruption in the air; this is a [vague](/Rules.aspx?ID=413) sense that allows you to know when such a creature is within 60 feet of you, though you can't precisely pinpoint their location with this sense. As normal for a vague sense, it can be fooled, but undead might not realize they need to take precautions against it. This sense also extends to living creatures who have the [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42) ability, like [dhampir](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=23).

 You deal an additional 1 cold damage with all Strikes made with an ice-crafted weapon. If you have the [Crown of the Saumen Kar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3416) feat, you deal an additional 1 cold damage with your unarmed horns Strikes.

<title level="2">Frostbite Runes leads to...</title>
[Bound in Ice](/Feats.aspx?ID=3419), [Curse of the Saumen Kar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3418)
name: Frostbite Runes

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Curse of the Saumen Kar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3418) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 11

**Archetype** [Pactbound Initiate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=167)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Frostbite Runes](/Feats.aspx?ID=3417)

</column>

---

You activate the runes on your body to create an icy prison around a Huge or smaller creature you can see within 60 feet. The prison is a sphere made of unmelting ice, just large enough to fit your target. The target, and any creatures sharing its space, must attempt a Reflex save, with a DC using the higher of your class DC and spell DC. A creature that fails becomes trapped inside the prison. If it succeeds, the creature is pushed outside the prison into a space of its choice. If the target shares its space with a gargantuan creature, the effect fails.

 The dome has AC 10, Hardness 20, and 40 Hit Points; it's immune to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), critical hits, and precision damage. The prison lasts until destroyed, until you use this ability to create a new prison, or until you die.
name: Curse of the Saumen Kar

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bound in Ice](/Feats.aspx?ID=3419) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 11

**Archetype** [Pactbound Initiate](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=167)

**Prerequisites**
[Frostbite Runes](/Feats.aspx?ID=3417)

</column>

---

You become a hybrid of your ancestry and a saumen kar. Your size increases to Large, and your reach becomes 10 feet. Your [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) resistance increases to be equal to your level, and you gain [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) resistance equal to half your level.

 You also gain the Blizzard Evasion reaction.

 **Blizzard Evasion** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You take 40 or more physical damage from a single attack; **Effect** You discorporate into a whirling blizzard for up to 3 rounds. During this time, you gain resistance 10 to physical damage and weakness 10 to [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage. You gain a fly speed of 40 feet, but the only action you can take is to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94). The first time each round you enter another creature's space while discorporated, or the first time a creature enters your space, that creature takes 4d8 cold damage and must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher, or else be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). At the start of your turn, you can end this effect as a free action.
name: Bound in Ice

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Bard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=19" />
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />

<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=136" />

<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=323" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 185

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) or Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)

**Access**
You are a member of the College of Mysteries

</column>

---

Your face bears an intricate magical tattoo known as the _irezoko_, a badge of your understanding of your chosen field of magic and a recognition of your advancement within Absalom's College of Mysteries. Choose a class that you are a member of that grants you access to a focus pool. When you take this feat, it gains the trait that applies to your chosen class. Once per day, you may concentrate upon the pattern of your _irezoko_ to recover 1 Focus Point as a three-action activity.
name: Irezoko Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[All of the Animal](/Feats.aspx?ID=3422) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 78

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

</column>

---

You know how to make the best and most efficient use of a hunted animal's carcass. Choose a Large or larger corpse of an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) that died within the past day. While using Survival to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=27) near the carcass, you always receive enough food for yourself and one additional Medium creature, using up the meat from the carcass to do so. Since you're using all of the animal, others can't use the carcass's meat for any other purpose, including their own use of All of the Animal.

<title level="2">Skill Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 78

Characters can gain access to the following new skill feats throughout “Broken Tusk Moon.”

 **All of the Animal**: Head scout Wipa teaches this skill feat to characters who help her butcher an animal for meat, such as the moose at the beginning of the adventure or another animal slain throughout the course of the Broken Tusk following's journey.

 **Spirit Speaker**: Mammoth Lord Argakoa, also known as Argakoa the Songsinger, might teach a fraction of her tremendous skill to a young scout who joins her in song. 

**Tame Animal**: The young herd tender Imek has a natural way with animals. She is happy to teach what she knows to any characters who display a similar reverence toward living creatures, such as by peacefully resolving their encounter with the giant porcupine in Chapter 1 of this adventure. Although this feat is particularly useful to characters who want to recruit animal followers throughout the Quest for the Frozen Flame Adventure Path, it isn't strictly necessary for the party to succeed in their adventures. It can be a great boon to parties taking part in other quests, as well.

**Related Feats**: [Spirit Speaker](/Feats.aspx?ID=3423), [Tame Animal](/Feats.aspx?ID=3424)
name: All of the Animal

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame **Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 78 **Prerequisites** Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) --- You know that when an animal dies, its spirit lingers briefly before moving on. Using low chants, body language that shows respect, and special movements that demonstrate to the spirit that you are an ally, you glean information from a spirit before it departs. This is a 1-minute activity. You must begin the activity while adjacent to the body of an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) or [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20) that died within the last 1 minute. As part of the activity, you ask one simple question of the spirit. Attempt a Nature check at a DC determined by the GM (normally, this is the [appropriate DC for the level of the animal or beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) when it was alive). Regardless of the result of your check, the spirit immediately departs and can't be used for Spirit Speaker again. If the creature had the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) or [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition when it died, you can't get a result better than a critical failure. Only one creature can use Spirit Speaker on a given dead animal, as the effort takes the spirit's full attention. **Critical Success** The spirit answers you truthfully. The spirit's answer is likely to be terse or cryptic compared to normal communication with an animal, and it can pertain only to things the creature directly experienced or considered important. The spirit doesn't remember anything that happened more than 1 day ago.
**Success** As critical success, but the spirit doesn't recall anything that happened more than 1 hour ago.
**Failure** The spirit doesn't answer you.
**Critical Failure** The spirit is wracked with misery or bewilderment and gives you an erroneous answer. Skill Feats **Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 78 Characters can gain access to the following new skill feats throughout “Broken Tusk Moon.” **All of the Animal**: Head scout Wipa teaches this skill feat to characters who help her butcher an animal for meat, such as the moose at the beginning of the adventure or another animal slain throughout the course of the Broken Tusk following's journey. **Spirit Speaker**: Mammoth Lord Argakoa, also known as Argakoa the Songsinger, might teach a fraction of her tremendous skill to a young scout who joins her in song. **Tame Animal**: The young herd tender Imek has a natural way with animals. She is happy to teach what she knows to any characters who display a similar reverence toward living creatures, such as by peacefully resolving their encounter with the giant porcupine in Chapter 1 of this adventure. Although this feat is particularly useful to characters who want to recruit animal followers throughout the Quest for the Frozen Flame Adventure Path, it isn't strictly necessary for the party to succeed in their adventures. It can be a great boon to parties taking part in other quests, as well. **Related Feats**: [All of the Animal](/Feats.aspx?ID=3422), [Tame Animal](/Feats.aspx?ID=3424)
name: Spirit Speaker

May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame **Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 78 **Prerequisites** Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) --- Animals born and raised far from humanoid settlements rarely get along with two-legged beings, whom they assume to be dangerous predators. In spite of this, you know how to approach and entreat a wild animal so that it's more receptive to your presence and requests. To use this feat, choose a wild [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) that isn't actively hostile to you and which isn't confined to an area smaller than its Speed in diameter (animals held in place or trapped are typically too scared to be tamed). Attempt a Nature check against a DC determined by the GM; this is usually the animal's Will DC, but circumstances might adjust the precise DC. This attempt usually takes an hour, but you can perform it in 10 minutes if you have expert proficiency in Nature, 1 minute if you have master proficiency, or as a three-action activity if you have legendary proficiency. **Critical Success** You form a friendly bond with the animal. As long as it isn't starving, the animal won't attack you or creatures obviously allied with you, even if they're creatures the animal would normally prey upon. This effect is permanent.
**Success** As critical success, but the effect lasts for 1 month. If you or an ally teach the animal a basic action during that time with the [Train Animal](/Feats.aspx?ID=856) skill feat, the effect is permanent.
**Failure** You fail to make any kind of meaningful impression on the animal.
**Critical Failure** The animal does its best to attack you and is immune to Tame Animal for 1 month. Skill Feats **Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 78 Characters can gain access to the following new skill feats throughout “Broken Tusk Moon.” **All of the Animal**: Head scout Wipa teaches this skill feat to characters who help her butcher an animal for meat, such as the moose at the beginning of the adventure or another animal slain throughout the course of the Broken Tusk following's journey. **Spirit Speaker**: Mammoth Lord Argakoa, also known as Argakoa the Songsinger, might teach a fraction of her tremendous skill to a young scout who joins her in song. **Tame Animal**: The young herd tender Imek has a natural way with animals. She is happy to teach what she knows to any characters who display a similar reverence toward living creatures, such as by peacefully resolving their encounter with the giant porcupine in Chapter 1 of this adventure. Although this feat is particularly useful to characters who want to recruit animal followers throughout the Quest for the Frozen Flame Adventure Path, it isn't strictly necessary for the party to succeed in their adventures. It can be a great boon to parties taking part in other quests, as well. **Related Feats**: [All of the Animal](/Feats.aspx?ID=3422), [Spirit Speaker](/Feats.aspx?ID=3423)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Game Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3425) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 81

**Archetype** [Game Hunter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=169)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

</column>

---

You are skilled at tracking big game animals and similar creatures. You gain the [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=10) action, but you can designate only [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), and [dragons](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) as prey. In addition to the other benefits of Hunt Prey, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks against your hunted prey.

 When you succeed at a Strike against your hunted prey while it's [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), it must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC. On a failure, the prey's Speeds are each reduced by 10 feet for 1 round; on a critical failure, the duration of this effect is 1 minute. The prey is then temporarily immune to this effect for 10 minutes.

 If you already have Hunt Prey, you become an expert in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). You apply the Stealth bonus and the Speed reduction only when your prey is an animal, beast, or dragon.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the game hunter archetype.

<title level="2">Game Hunter Dedication leads to...</title>
[Big Game Trapper](/Feats.aspx?ID=3426), [Keep Pace](/Feats.aspx?ID=3427), [Quick Positioning](/Feats.aspx?ID=3428)
name: Game Hunter Dedication

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Keep Pace](/Feats.aspx?ID=3427) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 81

**Archetype** [Game Hunter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=169)

**Prerequisites**
[Game Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3425)

**Trigger**
Your prey is within reach and attempts to move away from you.

</column>

---

When your hunted prey tries to bolt, you follow. You Stride up to your Speed, following your hunted prey and keeping it in reach throughout its movement until it stops moving or you've moved your full Speed. You can use Keep Pace to [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) instead of Stride if you have the appropriate movement type.
name: Keep Pace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Pyrophilic Recovery](/Feats.aspx?ID=3429) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Leshy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=225" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 77

**Access**
Pine leshies have access to this feat

</column>

---

You rely on fire and extreme heat to sprout and regrow. Whenever you take fire damage, at the beginning of your next turn, you regain Hit Points equal to half your character level (minimum 1). The total number of Hit Points you recover in this way, including any adjustments that increase the effects of healing, can't exceed half the Hit Points you lost from the fire damage you took. For example, if an effect deals 15 fire damage and you have fire resistance 5, you would take 10 fire damage and Pyrophilic Recovery could recover at most 5 Hit Points, regardless of any other effects that increase healing. If you take fire damage more than once before your next turn, the Hit Points recovered from Pyrophilic Recovery are cumulative, though still no more than half the fire damage taken each time.
name: Pyrophilic Recovery

    pfs="Limited"

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Leshy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=225" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 77

**Access**
Pine leshies have access to this feat

</column>

---

Like evergreen trees, which stay healthy and vibrant from winter through summer, you too can adapt to a variety of extreme climates. You're unaffected by mild cold or heat environmental effects, and when you're in severe cold or heat, you take the effects of mild cold or heat instead (see [Table 10–13](/Rules.aspx?ID=642)). This doesn't protect you from extreme or incredible cold or heat.
name: Climate Adaptation

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

Your body is covered in sharp needles or spines which you can use to defend yourself. Each time you become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by a creature or an adjacent creature hits you with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack, you twist and thrash your body around, dealing 2 piercing damage to that creature. This damage increases to 3 at 9th level, 4 at 13th level, and 5 at 17th level.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Cunning Trickster Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3433) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 78

**Archetypes** [Druid](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=6), [Wizard](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=12) (Level 20), [Wizard](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=218) (Level 20)

**Prerequisites**
[Druid Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=701) or [Wizard Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=742)

</column>

---

Your mask carries the legacy of Verdant Spider, the Speaker of Needs, allowing you to deceive and outwit your foes. You can attempt to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) a target from up to 30 feet away, and if you succeed or critically succeed in your Feint attempt, the target of your Feint takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saving throws against your spells until the start of your next turn. If you don't have the [Conceal Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=640) feat, you gain it as a bonus feat, allowing you to disguise your spellcasting from others' eyes. If you already have the Conceal Spell feat, you can use Conceal Spell once per round as a free action instead of as a single action.

<title level="2">Masks of the Magic Warriors</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 78

In becoming Jatembe's new Magic Warriors, the heroes have unlocked secrets of magical potential that none have reached since the earliest Ten Magic Warriors walked the plazas of the Magaambya.

 On reaching 20th level, the heroes should have the option of taking one of these archetype feats, based on the type of animal they chose for their mask when they first created it in _Pathfinder Adventure Path #169: Kindled Magic_. These options are each themed to one of Jatembe's magic warriors, but if a hero's mask doesn't fit with a specific animal from this list or the player feels their animal embodies a different effect from the list, feel free to reflavor the feat names to fit the hero's spirit face. For instance, the Cunning Trickster mask could easily be a rodent or a fox, not just a spider.

**Related Feats**: [Emancipator's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3434), [Grand Medic's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3435), [Protective Spirit Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3436), [Sky Master Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3437), [Stalking Feline Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3438), [Storyteller's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3439), [Thick Hide Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3440), [Tireless Guide's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3441), [Vigilant Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3442)
name: Cunning Trickster Mask

>
[Emancipator's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3434) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

[Druid Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=701) or [Wizard Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=742)

</column>

---

Your mask grants you the abilities of Whistling Kite, the Vigilant Seer, who emancipated thousands. When you are attempting a check to free someone else from an effect that [immobilizes](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), [paralyzes](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), or [controls](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) them, roll twice and take the higher result; this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect. The GM might decide that this fortune effect applies when attempting checks to remove other conditions or effects to free another. Immediately after you Cast a non-cantrip Spell that targets only one creature, you can choose to permit the target to make an attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).

<title level="2">Masks of the Magic Warriors</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 78

These options are each themed to one of Jatembe's magic warriors, but if a hero's mask doesn't fit with a specific animal from this list or the player feels their animal embodies a different effect from the list, feel free to reflavor the feat names to fit the hero's spirit face. For instance, the Cunning Trickster mask could easily be a rodent or a fox, not just a spider.

**Related Feats**: [Cunning Trickster Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3433), [Grand Medic's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3435), [Protective Spirit Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3436), [Sky Master Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3437), [Stalking Feline Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3438), [Storyteller's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3439), [Thick Hide Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3440), [Tireless Guide's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3441), [Vigilant Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3442)
name: Emancipator's Mask

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>

---

Your mask carries the protective instincts of White Bull, the Horn Forger, and no one will hurt your allies while you have anything to say about it. Immediately after you Cast a non-cantrip Spell that targets only one ally, you can choose to have the spell also give that creature a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. You also gain the Protector's Interdiction reaction.

**Protector's Interdiction** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An adjacent ally is hit or critically hit by an attack; **Effect** You interpose yourself between your ally and the attack. The attack hits you instead, or critically hits you if it critically hit your ally.

<title level="2">Masks of the Magic Warriors</title>

    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Stalking Feline Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3438) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 78

**Archetypes** [Druid](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=6), [Wizard](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=12) (Level 20), [Wizard](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=218) (Level 20)

**Prerequisites**
[Druid Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=701) or [Wizard Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=742)

</column>

---

Like Azure Leopard, the Patient Warden, your mask makes you most at home in the night and teaches you how to use the darkness to hunt. You gain [greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415). Immediately after you Cast a non-cantrip Spell with the [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) or [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) trait, you can attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) as a free action. You must still meet the usual requirements of the action you choose, such as having cover or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to Hide and being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to Sneak. 

<title level="2">Masks of the Magic Warriors</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 78

In becoming Jatembe's new Magic Warriors, the heroes have unlocked secrets of magical potential that none have reached since the earliest Ten Magic Warriors walked the plazas of the Magaambya.

 On reaching 20th level, the heroes should have the option of taking one of these archetype feats, based on the type of animal they chose for their mask when they first created it in _Pathfinder Adventure Path #169: Kindled Magic_. These options are each themed to one of Jatembe's magic warriors, but if a hero's mask doesn't fit with a specific animal from this list or the player feels their animal embodies a different effect from the list, feel free to reflavor the feat names to fit the hero's spirit face. For instance, the Cunning Trickster mask could easily be a rodent or a fox, not just a spider.

**Related Feats**: [Cunning Trickster Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3433), [Emancipator's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3434), [Grand Medic's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3435), [Protective Spirit Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3436), [Sky Master Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3437), [Storyteller's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3439), [Thick Hide Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3440), [Tireless Guide's Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3441), [Vigilant Mask](/Feats.aspx?ID=3442)
name: Stalking Feline Mask

When they follow you when you use a trained skill, their circumstance bonus is +1. Immediately after you Cast a non-cantrip Spell that targets only one ally, that ally can Stride as a free action.

**Prerequisites**

</column>

---

Your mask grants the extraordinary magical senses of Carmine Jaws, the Hyena who Looks Between. You gain [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) as a [precise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2406)  to a range of 60 feet. If you already have scent as a precise sense, increase its range by 60 feet. You can sometimes catch a faint whiff of magic itself. Immediately after you Cast a non-cantrip Spell, your scent extends between worlds, like Carmine Jaws's own. This causes you to be able to smell [ethereal](/Traits.aspx?ID=63) creatures, those in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space with an aperture that's within your scent range, [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures and objects, and other creatures and objects that are insubstantial or not fully in phase with reality. Unlike your normal scent, this is an [imprecise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=413).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mammoth Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3443) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 77

**Archetype** [Mammoth Lord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=170)

**Prerequisites**
[Ride](/Feats.aspx?ID=835)

</column>

---

You've undertaken the ultimate challenge of the Mammoth Lords and tamed a megafauna. You gain a megafauna you tamed as a [young animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=151). Your megafauna animal companion must be a megafauna appropriate to the Realm of the Mammoth Lords; see the What Counts as Megafauna? sidebar for guidance and examples. While an animal companion usually starts as Small, you can begin with a Medium version of that animal (changing no statistics other than its size).

 Contrary to the usual rules for animal companions, this feat can give you a second animal companion. If you have more than one animal companion, you can adventure with only one of them at a time. You can switch between them like a [beastmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=50) does. You gain the Call Companion action, as the beastmaster archetype, and you follow all the other rules found in the Call Companion sidebar.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from this archetype.

<title level="2">Mammoth Lord Dedication leads to...</title>
[Gigantic Megafauna Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=3452), [Mammoth Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=3444), [Mature Megafauna Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=3445), [Megafauna Veterinarian](/Feats.aspx?ID=3446), [Specialized Megafauna Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=3453), [Speech of the Mammoth Lords](/Feats.aspx?ID=3447), [Staggering Blow](/Feats.aspx?ID=3450), [Stalwart Mind](/Feats.aspx?ID=3449), [Unbreakable Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=3451)
name: Mammoth Lord Dedication

You've raised your megafauna companion to be a more powerful force on the battlefield than most of its kind. The companion you gained through the Mammoth Lord Dedication feat becomes a [mature animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=155), granting it additional capabilities. During an encounter, even if you don't use the [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) action, your megafauna animal companion can still use 1 action on your turn to Stride or Strike.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Megafauna Veterinarian](/Feats.aspx?ID=3446) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #177: Burning Tundra](/Sources.aspx?ID=116) pg. 78

**Archetype** [Mammoth Lord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=170)

**Prerequisites**
[Mammoth Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3443); Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

</column>

---

By heeding the wise advice of elders in your following— and aided by your own experience—you've learned how to provide medical care to the megafauna of the Mammoth Lord followings. You can attempt a [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) check instead of a [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check for any of Medicine's trained and untrained uses, provided the subject of your medical care is a megafauna. If you're attempting an action that requires a particular proficiency rank in Medicine (such as [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57)), use your proficiency rank in Nature instead. If the subject of your care is your megafauna and you roll a success on your check, you get a critical success instead.
name: Megafauna Veterinarian

You and your megafauna companion are bound together; as long as you live, it's much harder for your companion to die. If your megafauna companion gains the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) condition, it automatically succeeds at the recovery check to stabilize at 0 Hit Points on its turn. Additionally, your megafauna companion only dies at dying 5 rather than dying 4. Finally, the first time each day your megafauna companion would reach dying 5 and die, as long as you're still alive, your companion's dying value decreases to 0 instead.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Exorcist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3454) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 22

**Archetype** [Exorcist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=171)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) or Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

You've learned to attract, quell, and purify spirits, housing them in a special receptacle called a _spirit dwelling_ until they're ready to move on. Through the power of prayers or ritual incantations, one mundane object in your possession becomes a _spirit dwelling_. Your _spirit dwelling_ functions as a lure for lost spirits weakened by their time on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). Each day during your daily preparations, your _spirit dwelling_ attracts a spirit wisp who comes to dwell inside. If your _spirit dwelling_ contains no wisps, you can spend 10 minutes in a minor ritual to cast your _spirit dwelling_ around an area and attract another wisp. You can also capture stronger spirit remnants from vanquished [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) spirits, as explained in the [Spirit Dwellings and Remnants](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=171) sidebar. As long as your _spirit dwelling_ contains any spirits, it glows faintly, casting dim light in a 10-foot radius.

 As an exorcist, you do more than just collect spirits: you also help rid them of their burdens and lingering resentments, aiding their transition from the Material Plane. Every day, before your daily preparations, any spirit wisps and remnants remaining within your _spirit dwelling_ from the previous day are purified and can join the River of Souls in their final journey to [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15).

 You can also learn abilities that let you purify a spirit in your _spirit dwelling_ immediately in a cathartic surge, granting you a helpful effect as they depart for the afterlife. Any actions you gain from the exorcist archetype gain either the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) or [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120) trait, depending on whether you used Occultism or Religion to qualify for Exorcist Dedication.

 Spirit's Mercy is the simplest of the purifications.

 **Spirit's Mercy** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** You take [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) or [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) damage, or you take any type of damage caused by a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=88), [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51), or other [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) undead; **Cost** 1 spirit wisp or remnant; **Effect** You purify a spirit by having it perform a final act of mercy to lessen the damage caused by another spirit. This grants you resistance to positive and negative damage (or resistance to all damage if caused by a haunt or incorporeal undead) against the triggering effect. If you expend a spirit wisp, the resistance is equal to twice your level. If you expend a spirit remnant, the resistance is equal to three times the level of the incorporeal undead or haunt from which you gained the remnant.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the exorcist archetype.

<title level="2">Exorcist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cast Out](/Feats.aspx?ID=3455), [Enticing Dwelling](/Feats.aspx?ID=3458), [Spirit's Absolution](/Feats.aspx?ID=3456), [Spirit's Anguish](/Feats.aspx?ID=3457)
name: Exorcist Dedication

You attempt to cast a malevolent entity out of the creature or object it's possessing. Roll a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against the [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129) effect; you must be within 30 feet of the possessed creature or object to do so. Your counteract level is half your level rounded up, and your counteract modifier is your [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) modifier, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) modifier, or spell DC – 10, whichever is highest. If you succeed, the entity is driven out and can't attempt to possess that creature or object for 1 week. If you fail, the possessing creature is immune to your Cast Out for a year and a day.
name: Cast Out

>
[Hallowed Necromancer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3459) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 24

**Archetype** [Hallowed Necromancer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=172)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); good alignment, able to cast spells using spell slots, able to cast at least one [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) spell

</column>

---

You've studied techniques allowing you to blend so-called hallowed necromancy into your own spellcasting to bolster the living and destroy the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). You gain the [_hallowed ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1103) focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell. This feat grants a focus pool of 1 Focus Point that you can recover using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity. You can Refocus by meditating or praying to connect yourself to conduits of positive energy. Focus spells from the hallowed necromancer archetype have the same tradition as your spell slots.

 Casting spells that create, heal, or otherwise aid or promote undead is anathema to you, as is cooperation with undead. Using negative energy isn't anathema, as it serves a natural purpose, but twisting that negative energy for creation is blasphemous to you. If you perform acts that are anathema to your principles, you lose access to all feats from this archetype. These abilities can be regained only with an [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) ritual.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the hallowed necromancer archetype.

<title level="2">Hallowed Necromancer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Advanced Hallowed Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=3463), [Consecrated Aura](/Feats.aspx?ID=3465), [Death Warden](/Feats.aspx?ID=3462), [Hallowed Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=3460), [Positive Luminance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3464), [Sacred Spells](/Feats.aspx?ID=3461)
name: Hallowed Necromancer Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); good alignment, able to cast spells using spell slots, able to cast at least one [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) spell
