**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 25 **Archetype** [Hallowed Necromancer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=172) **Prerequisites** [Hallowed Necromancer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3459) --- Add [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [_death ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=64), [_disrupt undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=79), [_disrupting weapons_](/Spells.aspx?ID=80), [_holy cascade_](/Spells.aspx?ID=151), [_magic stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=703), and [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=326) to your spell list. These are all [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) spells for you, even if they normally have a different spell school. You can either prepare these spells or add them to your repertoire just like the spells normally on your tradition's spell list. When you gain this feat, you can retrain existing spells to replace them with ones on this list. In addition, your knowledge of the conduits to the [Positive Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3) allows you more flexibility with these spells. Choose one of these two benefits.
  • If you're a prepared spellcaster, you can spend 10 minutes to replace one of the spells you've prepared with a spell of the same level from the list of sacred spells. You can do this while [Refocusing](/Actions.aspx?ID=71).
  • If you're a spontaneous spellcaster with the signature spells class feature, add two of the spells from the list of sacred spells to your repertoire. They are signature spells for you, in addition to your normal signature spells. When you gain the ability to cast a new level of spells, you can choose to switch the spells from this feat to any other spells from the list.
name: Sacred Spells

    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Consecrated Aura](/Feats.aspx?ID=3465) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 25

**Archetype** [Hallowed Necromancer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=172)

**Prerequisites**
[Hallowed Necromancer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3459)

</column>

---

Your body radiates positive energy, making your very presence disorienting to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and encouraging them to move away from you. The effect plays upon the instincts flowing through their bodies from negative energy and can thus affect even [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) creatures. You gain an aura of positive energy in a 20-foot emanation. Each undead creature that ends its turn in your aura must succeed at a Will save against your spell DC or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

**Special** Your aura gains the trait of the magical tradition for the spells you used to qualify for Hallowed Necromancer Dedication.
name: Consecrated Aura

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Soul Warden Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3466) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 26

**Archetype** [Soul Warden](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=173)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) or Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); worships [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) or a psychopomp usher

</column>

---

You can take 10 minutes to emblazon Pharasma's holy sigil—a spiraling comet that represents the winding path a soul takes through its existence—upon a shield, tabard, banner, or other prominent object that your wear or wield. The symbol doesn't fade until 1 year has passed, but if you emblazon the symbol again, any symbol you previously emblazoned and any symbol already emblazoned on that item instantly disappears. The item becomes a [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of Pharasma and can be used as a divine focus while emblazoned.

 Whenever an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature or captive soul is within 100 feet of this object, the spiral glows, shedding dim blue light in a 10-foot radius. If the creature is hiding or the soul is hidden, it must succeed at a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check against your Perception DC to fool the sigil and prevent the spiral from glowing.

 In addition, you can cast [_disrupt undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=79) as a divine innate cantrip at will. As normal, a cantrip is automatically heightened to half your level rounded up. You gain access to the Cast a Spell activity if you didn't have it already. You're trained in divine spell attack rolls and spell DCs. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Wisdom.

 When you have at least two other feats from this archetype, the spiral glows with [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 10-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 10 feet). Feats that require a creature or object to be in the light of your spiral function whether it's in the bright or dim light.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the soul warden archetype.

<title level="2">Soul Warden Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cycle Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=3467), [Liberate Soul](/Feats.aspx?ID=3469), [Psychopomp Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3468), [Safeguard Soul](/Feats.aspx?ID=3472), [Spiral Sworn](/Feats.aspx?ID=3470)
name: Soul Warden Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) or Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); worships [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) or a psychopomp usher

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 27 **Archetype** [Soul Warden](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=173) **Prerequisites** [Soul Warden Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3466); familiar --- Your [familiar](Familiars.aspx) becomes a masked [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83)—an extraplanar guardian of the cycle of souls. This familiar continues to use all the same rules as other familiars, but one of its abilities must always be [speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=12) and it has the [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111) and [psychopomp](/Traits.aspx?ID=232) traits. As normal when a familiar must always have a certain ability, the speech ability counts against your familiar's abilities each day. You can select three familiar or master abilities each day, instead of two, but one must be one of the following psychopomp familiar abilities.
  • **Soul Sight** Your familiar gains [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) with a range of 30 feet.
  • **Spirit Touch** Your familiar can touch [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures. If you have the [spell delivery](/Familiars.aspx?ID=18) master ability from your familiar, any spell the familiar delivers with it gains the benefits of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune.
Psychopomp Familiar leads to... [Enhanced Psychopomp Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3471)
name: Psychopomp Familiar

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 27

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Soul Warden Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3466)

**Requirements**
Your spiral is glowing.

</column>

---

The light flows from your sigil of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) to shatter the bonds imprisoning a soul. You attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129) effect, or an effect, object, or spell that is imprisoning a soul (such as [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21), a _soul gem_, or a [devourer's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=612) devoured souls). You must be within 60 feet of the possessed creature or object, or of the receptacle imprisoning the soul. Your counteract level is half your level rounded up, and your counteract modifier is your divine spell DC – 10. If the attempt fails, you can't use Liberate Soul against that particular effect again unless circumstances have changed drastically, as determined by the GM.
name: Liberate Soul

<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />

You trace a spiral in the air while intoning prayers to [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), gaining her blessing for a time. For 3 rounds, your Strikes and spells deal additional damage against [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), creatures in possession of an imprisoned soul, or creatures you have witnessed create or command undead. You gain a status bonus to damage with your Strikes against these creatures equal to the number of weapon damage dice. Spells you cast from spell slots gain a status bonus to damage against these creatures equal to the level of the spell; this bonus applies only to spells that deal damage and don't have a duration. If your spiral is glowing, you can target a willing creature in its light. If you do, that creature gains the bonuses instead of you.

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 27 **Archetype** [Soul Warden](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=173) **Prerequisites** [Psychopomp Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=3468) --- Your psychopomp's continued service in [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) name brings it favor from the goddess. It might change appearance to look like a different, more powerful kind of psychopomp. You can select four familiar or master abilities each day, instead of two, but two must be from the Psychopomp Familiar feat or the following ability.
  • **Augury** Your [familiar](Familiars.aspx) can glimpse the strands of fate to give you a cryptic clue regarding your future. Your familiar can cast [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) once per day using your magical tradition and spell DC. You must be at least 8th level to select this ability.

You've girded your soul against outside interference. You gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129) effects, and effects that attempt to manipulate or steal your soul. You can't be transformed into an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) by any means. While your spiral is glowing, your allies in the light of the spiral gain this benefit as well.
name: Safeguard Soul

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Undead Slayer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3474) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 28

**Archetype** [Undead Slayer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=174)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

Your training has prepared you to recognize most [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) on sight. You become trained in two [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skills, each about a specific type of undead, or become an expert if you were already trained. You gain the Slayer's Identification free action.

 **Slayer's Identification** <actions string="Free Action" /> **Trigger** You roll initiative and can observe a creature you know is undead; **Effect** You attempt to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) to identify the undead creature with a +1 circumstance bonus. If you're a master in the skill you're using to Recall Knowledge, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus instead.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the undead slayer archetype.

<title level="2">Undead Slayer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Blessed Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=3475), [Frighten Undead](/Feats.aspx?ID=3479), [Gear Up](/Feats.aspx?ID=3480), [Hunter's Sanctum](/Feats.aspx?ID=3476), [Necromantic Resistance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3477), [Slayer's Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=3478)
name: Undead Slayer Dedication

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blessed Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=3475) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

**Archetype** [Undead Slayer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=174)

[Undead Slayer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3474)

**Trigger**
You fail a check to [Treat a Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=55) that was inflicted by an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) (such as ghoul fever or mummy rot).

</column>

---

Your experience battling undead has uniquely prepared you to fight the diseases they inflict on the survivors, and you know that leaving these maladies untreated for long will eventually lead to more undead. Reroll the failed check and use the new result.
name: Blessed Medicine

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hunter's Sanctum](/Feats.aspx?ID=3476) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 28

**Archetype** [Undead Slayer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=174)

**Prerequisites**
[Undead Slayer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3474)

</column>

---

You establish a safe house where you can prepare for your hunt. The sanctum is roughly the size of a 20-foot cube. This sanctum is in a location you have access to and can be part of a larger structure, such as the basement of a temple or hidden room of a library. Setting up or moving your sanctum takes a week of downtime. Your sanctum protects objects and people inside it from magical detection and the prying eyes of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). This has the effects of [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209), using your [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) modifier for the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC and half your level rounded up for the counteract level. Additionally, undead attempting to locate the entrance to your sanctum via mundane Perception must succeed against either your Religion DC or the normal DC to find the sanctum, whichever is higher.
name: Hunter's Sanctum

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Slayer's Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=3478) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 28

**Archetype** [Undead Slayer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=174)

**Prerequisites**
[Undead Slayer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3474)

</column>

---

You've dedicated yourself to studying ancient techniques and memorized minor prayers against [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) to swiftly dispatch them. Make a Strike against a creature you know is undead. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, you deal one extra die of positive damage, with the same die size as the weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack you used. Any further Strikes you make against the same creature before the start of your next turn using the same weapon or unarmed attack deal extra positive damage equal to the number of weapon dice. As normal, the positive damage harms only undead and creatures with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42).

 If you're at least 10th level, increase this to two extra dice, and if you're at least 18th level, increase it to three extra dice.

<title level="2">Slayer's Strike leads to...</title>
[Slayer's Blessing](/Feats.aspx?ID=3481)
name: Slayer's Strike

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Frighten Undead](/Feats.aspx?ID=3479) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 29

**Prerequisites**
[Undead Slayer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3474); Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

---

Your very presence chills creatures of the night down to their core and you can use the spiritual power and faithful life force of religious verses to frighten even [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). Attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) an undead target using your [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) modifier instead of your [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) modifier if it's higher. If you use your Religion modifier, the Demoralize action loses the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits, as your faith connects to the undead on an instinctual level.

<title level="2">Frighten Undead leads to...</title>
[Slayer's Presence](/Feats.aspx?ID=3482)
name: Frighten Undead

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gear Up](/Feats.aspx?ID=3480) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 29

**Archetype** [Undead Slayer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=174)

**Prerequisites**
[Undead Slayer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3474)

</column>

---

Whether it's wooden stakes, holy water, or more unusual tools, you always have just the right thing to handle an [undead's](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) unusual weaknesses and vulnerabilities. You gain the [Prescient Planner](/Feats.aspx?ID=2143) and [Prescient Consumable](/Feats.aspx?ID=2142) feats. When using Prescient Planner specifically to pull out an item you need to deal with an undead's weakness or vulnerability, you can pull out the item as a 2-action activity to remove your backpack (or similar container) and draw the item, rather than taking 1 minute. If you have the Hunter's Sanctum feat, you keep your sanctum well stocked with the basics and can spend 1 hour in your sanctum refreshing your supplies in order to meet the requirements for Prescient Planner, even if you haven't been able to purchase goods.
name: Gear Up

**Prerequisites**
[Slayer's Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=3478)

</column>

---

You carry scraps of materials dangerous to undead and can apply them to your weapon. When you use Slayer's Strike, before making the Strike, you can choose either the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune or a precious material: [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375), [darkwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=376), or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379). Any Strike that gets extra positive damage from this use of Slayer's Strike also gets the benefits of the rune or counts as that material.

 At 14th level, add [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) and [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=377) to the list of materials you can choose from.
name: Slayer's Blessing

**Special** This feat has the trait matching your spellcasting tradition.

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 33 **Archetype** [Reanimator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=175) (Level 12) **Prerequisites** ability to create or control [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160); [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) with a negative font, [oracle](/Classes.aspx?ID=14) of [bones](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=3), or [necromancer](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7) [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=12) --- Necromantic energy demonstrates your power over undead, creating a demeanor that commands their fear and respect. Undead creatures can immediately sense your Necromancer's Visage and tend to act accordingly. If you take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against an undead creature, it becomes permanently immune to your Necromancer's Visage, and your Necromancer's Visage ends. You can spend 10 minutes to restore your Necromancer's Visage, though any creatures that became permanently immune remain so. Each time an undead creature 2 or more levels lower than you attacks you, it must attempt a Will save against your spell DC. If you're also affected by [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) or a similar effect, only one affects the attacker (you choose which).
**Critical Success** The creature is permanently immune to your Necromancer's Visage.
**Success** The creature can attempt its attack and any other attacks against you this turn.
**Failure** The creature can't attack you and wastes the action. It can't attempt further attacks against you this turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature wastes the action and can't attempt to attack you as long as your Necromancer's Visage persists. **Special** This feat has the trait matching your spellcasting tradition.
name: Necromancer's Visage
prerequisite_markdown: ability to create or control [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160); [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) with a negative font, [oracle](/Classes.aspx?ID=14) of [bones](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=3), or [necromancer](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7) [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=12)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sepulchral Sublimation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3485) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=319" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 33

**Archetype** [Reanimator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=175) (Level 14)

**Prerequisites**
ability to create or control [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160); [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) with a negative font, [oracle](/Classes.aspx?ID=14) of [bones](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=3), or [necromancer](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7) [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=12)

**Requirements**
You have at least one undead [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) that you permanently control.

</column>

---

You repurpose the negative energy that flows through your minions at a moment's notice. Instantly destroy one undead minion within 60 feet that is permanently under your control. If your next action is to cast a [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) spell from a spell slot, you don't expend the slot. The spell's level must be half the destroyed minion's level or lower and the spell can't have a duration.

**Special** This feat has the trait matching your spellcasting tradition.
name: Sepulchral Sublimation
prerequisite_markdown: ability to create or control [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160); [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) with a negative font, [oracle](/Classes.aspx?ID=14) of [bones](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=3), or [necromancer](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7) [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=12)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Reanimator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3486) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 34

**Archetype** [Reanimator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=175)

**Prerequisites**
able to cast [_animate dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=666) with a spell slot

</column>

---

You have dedicated your studies to the art of raising and commanding undead. If you're a spontaneous spellcaster with _animate dead_ in your repertoire, it becomes a signature spell in addition to your usual signature spells. If you're a prepared spellcaster with _animate dead_ in your spells known, spellbook, or the like, you can spend 10 minutes contemplating undeath to replace one of the spells you've prepared in your spell slots with an animate dead spell of the same level. In addition, if you're able to target the mostly intact remains of an appropriate type of dead creature when casting _animate dead_, the undead you animate gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, AC, saving throws, and skill checks for the duration of _animate dead_.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the reanimator archetype.

<title level="2">Reanimator Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bonds of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=3489), [Deathly Secrets](/Feats.aspx?ID=3487), [Macabre Virtuoso](/Feats.aspx?ID=3488)
name: Reanimator Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: able to cast [_animate dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=666) with a spell slot

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Deathly Secrets](/Feats.aspx?ID=3487) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 34

**Archetype** [Reanimator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=175)

**Prerequisites**
[Reanimator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3486)

</column>

---

No means of creating or ruling the undead is beyond your grasp, even those of magical traditions not your own. You learn either the [_eyes of the dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1104) or [_subjugate undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1106) focus spell. If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can Refocus by meditating over the earthly remains of a dead sentient creature. The rules for focus spells appear [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=276). Focus spells granted by the reanimator archetype are from the same tradition as those used to meet the archetype's prerequisite.

**Special** You can select this feat more than once. Each time, you learn a different spell and your focus pool increases by 1 Focus Point.

<title level="2">Deathly Secrets leads to...</title>
[Greater Deathly Secrets](/Feats.aspx?ID=3490), [Master of the Dead](/Feats.aspx?ID=3491)
name: Deathly Secrets

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Macabre Virtuoso](/Feats.aspx?ID=3488) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 34

**Archetype** [Reanimator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=175)

**Prerequisites**
[Reanimator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3486); Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

Any necromancer can create [zombie](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=103) and [skeleton](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=92), but you know the secrets behind the creation of far more advanced undead monstrosities. You learn the [_create undead_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) rituals to create two types of common [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) for which you meet the prerequisites. (Your GM might give you access to additional types of undead.) You can't teach these rituals to anyone else and you can't participate in either ritual while allowing someone else to serve as the primary caster unless they also know the ritual. When you reach a new level, you can change either or both of these rituals to a different type of undead for which you now meet the prerequisites.

 When acting as primary caster, you can perform all _create undead_ rituals in 4 hours instead of 1 day and you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your primary check. You can also perform the ritual without the aid of a secondary caster, in which case you attempt the secondary check.

**Special** You can select this feat more than once. Each time, you learn the rituals to create two more types of undead creatures.
name: Macabre Virtuoso
prerequisite_markdown: [Reanimator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3486); Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bonds of Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=3489) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 35

**Archetype** [Reanimator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=175)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Reanimator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3486)

**Requirements**
You have cast [_animate dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=666) this turn.

</column>

---

You sway with the motions of your spells that animate the dead, channeling your power to sustain two at the same time. You Sustain your previous casting of _animate dead_, binding the necromantic energy of the two spells together with metaphysical twine. On subsequent rounds, you can Sustain both _animate dead_ spells with a single action to Sustain the Spell. This benefit lasts until either of the spells ends.
name: Bonds of Death
requirement_markdown: You have cast [_animate dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=666) this turn.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Undead Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3492) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 41

**Archetype** [Undead Master](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=176)

**Prerequisites**
evil alignment

</column>

---

You gain the services of an [undead companion](Companions.aspx?Type=Undead) that travels with you and obeys your commands. The rules for undead companions are under [Undead Companions](/Rules.aspx?ID=1690) and utilize those for [animal companions](/Rules.aspx?ID=149).

 Contrary to the usual rules for companions, as an undead master it's possible for you to have more than one undead companion at a time. However, only one of those companions, your “active companion,” follows you during exploration and encounters; the rest are reduced to an “inactive” state that can be easily tucked into an inventory or stored in a nearby crypt. If you ever have more than one undead companion, you gain the Call Companion action, with your undead companions taking the place of animal companions.

<document level="2" id="action-601" />

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the undead master archetype.

<title level="2">Undead Master Dedication leads to...</title>
[Guardian Ghosts](/Feats.aspx?ID=3493), [Their Master's Call](/Feats.aspx?ID=3494)
name: Undead Master Dedication

    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Guardian Ghosts](/Feats.aspx?ID=3493) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 41

**Archetype** [Undead Master](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=176)

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Undead Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3492)

**Trigger**
You would take damage from a Strike while one of your undead companions is adjacent to you.

</column>

---

You magically divert some of the harm from an incoming attack away from yourself, forcing your undead companion to take it in your stead. Reduce the damage you take from the triggering Strike by 2 + your level. Your adjacent undead companion loses a number of Hit Points equal to the amount of damage reduced. If you have more than one adjacent undead companion, you choose which one loses Hit Points. You can't divert the damage to a temporary fragment from Their Master's Call.
name: Guardian Ghosts

    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Their Master's Call](/Feats.aspx?ID=3494) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />

**Prerequisites**
[Undead Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3492); Call Companion, at least one inactive undead companion whose remains or anchor are currently in your possession.

</column>

---

In a pinch, you pull one of your inactive companions from their rest to provide a companion's support benefit. A fragment of your companion materializes or assembles itself in an unoccupied square of your choice within 30 feet of you, grants you its support benefit, and then disappears on your next turn. If it takes any damage before your next turn, it disappears or collapses (as applicable) and the support benefit ends immediately.
name: Their Master's Call
prerequisite_markdown: [Undead Master Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3492); Call Companion, at least one inactive undead companion whose remains or anchor are currently in your possession.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stitch Flesh](/Feats.aspx?ID=3495) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 45

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

You can use [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57) to restore Hit Points to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures, not just living ones. The techniques you use to do so vary, but all require sutures, bandages, and other tools included in [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25). Some conditions that might raise the DC of treating undead's wounds differ from that of living creatures. For instance, the GM might increase the DC if the undead being treated is in a church of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) or the wounds were caused by powerful positive energy.
name: Stitch Flesh

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghost Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3496) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 46

**Archetype** [Ghost](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=177)

**Prerequisites**
You died and returned as a ghost.

</column>

---

You have risen as a shell of your former self, a spirit of mist and anguish. You gain the [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149), and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) traits, and the [basic undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1694). Your undead craving is to settle your unfinished business. You also gain the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) trait except you can't pass through solid objects unless you select the Pass Through feat. Being a ghost has the following major effects.

 **Floating** You can float but are still tethered to the ground. Replace your land Speed with an equal fly Speed. You can't rise more than a few inches above the ground when you [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94). This means you can move above many types of difficult or hazardous terrain without moving slowly or being damaged, even though you can't fly without limit. You can [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36), [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37), and take similar actions, and use your fly Speed for any calculations that would normally require your land Speed.

 **Items** You can transmute physical items to make them part of your incorporeal form. This requires spending 10 minutes with the items within reach, during which you transform the items into part of your form; you can return items you already have incorporated to a corporeal state at the same time. The items retain all their runes and other abilities, need to be invested normally if they have the [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=98) trait, and need to be worn, held, or stowed appropriately. Once you've incorporated the items, you and other incorporeal creatures can use them normally—you can Interact with them, Release them, and so on. Your incorporated weapons gain the benefits of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune, allowing you to use them normally against both corporeal and incorporeal creatures. Incorporated items become corporeal again only if you transmute them back or are destroyed, in which case, they drop to the ground under you.

 **Attacks** Your unarmed attacks become [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) and deal negative damage instead of their normal type.

 **Strength** Unlike most incorporeal creatures, your Strength modifier is not –5; you keep the same Strength score you had before you became a ghost, though you can only attempt Strength-based skill checks—typically [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks—against other incorporeal creatures, as normal for an incorporeal creature. Against incorporeal creatures, use your Strength normally to determine the results of Athletics checks, Strikes with melee weapons, and any other checks or damage rolls dependent on Strength.

 **Ties that Bind** When you become a ghost, work with your GM to choose a bound site and unfinished business, both of which matter for your character's story, as well as some ghost archetype feats. Your bound site tethers you to the physical world and is typically either a location important to you in life or the place where you died. Unfinished business keeps you from passing to the afterlife. If someone resolves your unfinished business, you decide whether to accept the change and pass on, or to fight it. If you pass on, you get a few minutes to say your goodbyes, and then move into the River of Souls and the afterlife. Your character ceases to be. If you fight the change, you remain, though you and the GM might determine a new unfinished business. If you are physically destroyed, you cease your existence as a ghost but still might not be able to pass on to the afterlife if your unfinished business is incomplete. In this liminal state, you might come across strange energies and become another sort of creature, or anchor to a [summoner](/Classes.aspx?ID=18) and become an eidolon. 

<title level="2">Ghost Dedication leads to...</title>
[Frightful Moan](/Feats.aspx?ID=3497), [Ghost Flight](/Feats.aspx?ID=3500), [Ghostly Grasp (Ghost)](/Feats.aspx?ID=3499), [Ghostly Resistance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3498), [Headless Haunt](/Feats.aspx?ID=7699), [Pass Through](/Feats.aspx?ID=3501), [Rejuvenation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3502)
name: Ghost Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: You died and returned as a ghost.

[Ghostly Resistance](/Feats.aspx?ID=3498) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 47

**Archetype** [Ghost](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=177)

**Prerequisites**
[Ghost Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3496)

</column>

---

Your ghostly form becomes innately weaker but also gains resistance to many forms of damage. Your maximum HP is reduced by your level. You gain resistance 1 to all damage except for [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128), and any damage done by a weapon with the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) rune (or any other source that acts like a _ghost touch_ rune). This resistance increases to 2 if the source is non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103).

 At 10th level, the resistance increases to 2, or 4 if the source is non-magical. At 16th level, the resistance increases to 3, or 5 if the source is non-magical.
name: Ghostly Resistance

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 47 **Archetype** [Ghost](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=177) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Prerequisites** [Ghost Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3496); Master in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) --- Filtering your form through the substance of an object, you can pass through walls, doors, and more. You Fly up to your Speed. During this movement, you can try to move through one object. Attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check as you try to enter its space. The DC is typically 30 to move through a wall of up to 5 feet, 15 for an ordinary door, and 10 for thinner structures like windows; the GM might set the DC higher for especially dense materials like [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) or lead, or for barriers that are magically reinforced. You can't Pass Through an obstacle made of magical force, such as a [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=363). **Success** You move through the object, treating the square within it as difficult terrain. If you end your turn inside an object, you can move out of it only if you Pass Through again or use some other means of moving through a solid object. As normal for being [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222), starting your turn inside an object makes you [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for that turn.
**Failure** Your movement ends, and you trigger reactions as if you moved out of the square you started in.
name: Pass Through

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghoul Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3504) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Ghoul](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=178)

**Prerequisites**
You were killed by ghoul fever.

</column>

---

You have succumbed to ghoul fever, dying and returning as a [ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52), an undead cursed with a never-ending hunger for dead flesh that slowly overrides your reason and willpower until you would do anything for the meal you crave. In addition to the personality changes, your body changes as well. Your ears become pointed, and your skin grows blueish and pale. Your fingernails sharpen into claws; your teeth become razor sharp, ready to tear flesh off the bone; and your tongue grows long and pointed.

 You gain the [ghoul](/Traits.aspx?ID=218) and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) traits and the [basic undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1694). Your undead craving is for the flesh of dead creatures; freshly dead corpses from sapient creatures fulfill your cravings much more than non-sapient or rotted corpses, but any dead creature can stave off the hunger for at least a little while. You gain two [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group. The first unarmed attack is a claw that deals 1d4 slashing damage with the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) traits, and the second unarmed attack is a set of jaws that deal 1d6 piercing damage and have the finesse trait.

 You gain the Consume Flesh action. Satisfying your craving is difficult. Consuming Flesh temporarily satiates you. Many ghoul feats have extra abilities or functions that can be used when you are satiated, though using those abilities also causes you to lose your satiated status. You can always Consume Flesh, but eating more than a normal amount has no further effect; a ghoul's hunger is satiated, or it is not.

 **Consume Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** You are adjacent to the corpse of a Small or larger creature that died in the last hour; **Effect** You devour a chunk of the corpse. You become satiated for 1 hour.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the ghoul archetype

<title level="2">Ghoul Dedication leads to...</title>
[Corpse Stench](/Feats.aspx?ID=3512), [Feverish Enzymes](/Feats.aspx?ID=3507), [Flailtongue](/Feats.aspx?ID=3888), [Glutton for Flesh](/Feats.aspx?ID=3511), [Grave Strength](/Feats.aspx?ID=3508), [Replenishing Consumption](/Feats.aspx?ID=3505), [Secret Eater](/Feats.aspx?ID=3890), [Sickening Bite](/Feats.aspx?ID=3510), [Swift Leap](/Feats.aspx?ID=3506), [Tunneling Claws](/Feats.aspx?ID=3891)
name: Ghoul Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: You were killed by ghoul fever.

The target or an adjacent ally can spend two Interact actions to squeeze the enzymes from the wound and remove the effect.

 If you are satiated, when you hit you can choose to end your satiation and boost your enzymes, increasing the duration to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 49

**Archetype** [Ghoul](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=178)

**Prerequisites**
[Ghoul Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3504)

</column>

---

The flesh you've consumed over the course of your existence as a ghoul has made you stronger, gifting you with strange insights from the minds of the sapient creatures you've devoured and bringing you closer to a state of undead perfection. You gain the [advanced undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1695). In addition, you gain a +5-foot status bonus to your Speed while you're satiated.
name: Grave Strength

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Paralyzing Slash](/Feats.aspx?ID=3509) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=93" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 49

**Archetype** [Ghoul](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=178)

**Prerequisites**
[Feverish Enzymes](/Feats.aspx?ID=3507)

</column>

---

Your enzymes can paralyze your foes rather than merely making it harder for them to heal their wounds. Strike with your claw or jaws. If you hit a living non-[elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) creature, it becomes [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Regardless of the result of the save, the creature is temporarily immune to Paralyzing Slash for 24 hours. A creature that becomes paralyzed can attempt a new save to end the paralysis at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.
name: Paralyzing Slash

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sickening Bite](/Feats.aspx?ID=3510) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Ghoul Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3504)

</column>

---

Your saliva causes severe nausea and mild fever in the living, weakening them so that you can more easily finish them off. When you critically hit a creature with your jaws, the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). This is a disease effect.

 If you are satiated, you can end your satiation to make the target sickened 1 on a regular hit instead.
name: Sickening Bite

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glutton for Flesh](/Feats.aspx?ID=3511) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Ghoul](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=178)

**Prerequisites**
[Ghoul Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3504)

</column>

---

You have developed a capacity to store more flesh within your gaunt body to leave yourself satiated longer. If you [Consume Flesh](/Feats.aspx?ID=3504) while satiated, you become fully satiated. You stay fully satiated for 3 hours, after which you become satiated for 1 hour. If you're fully satiated when an ability would end your satiation, you cease being fully satiated but remain satiated. If you have [Grave Strength](/Feats.aspx?ID=3508), you gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed when fully satiated instead of a +5-foot status bonus for being satiated.

<title level="2">Glutton for Flesh leads to...</title>
[Competitive Eater](/Feats.aspx?ID=3889)
name: Glutton for Flesh

    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Corpse Stench](/Feats.aspx?ID=3512) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 49

**Archetypes** [Ghoul](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=178), [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182) (Level 14)

**Prerequisites**
[Ghoul Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3504)

</column>

---

Your body exudes an overwhelming scent of decay in a 10-foot emanation, so putrid that it nauseates creates within that range. Any creature that starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher, or be [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (plus slowed 1 on a critical failure). While within the aura, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against disease and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to Corpse Stench for 1 minute.
name: Corpse Stench

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lich Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3513) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 50

**Archetype** [Lich](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=179)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); living creature, ability to cast 6th-level or higher spells from spell slots, completed ritual to become a lich and crafted a _soul cage_

</column>

---

After years of study and careful planning, you finally completed a _soul cage_ to house your soul and successfully performed a ritual to transform yourself into a lich. Now, neither death nor time can prevent you from pursuing your studies and achieving your grand ambitions. You gain the undead trait and the [basic undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1694). Your undead craving is for knowledge.

 You gain a unique [_soul cage_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545)—a magic item that houses your soul. Whenever you would die, your soul flees to the _soul cage_ to allow you to be rebuilt. As long as your soul cage exists, you can't truly be destroyed.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the lich archetype.

<title level="2">Lich Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bolster Soul Cage](/Feats.aspx?ID=3517), [Drain Soul Cage](/Feats.aspx?ID=3514), [Enshroud Soul Cage](/Feats.aspx?ID=3515), [Hand of the Lich](/Feats.aspx?ID=3516), [Spell Gem](/Feats.aspx?ID=3518)
name: Lich Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); living creature, ability to cast 6th-level or higher spells from spell slots, completed ritual to become a lich and crafted a _soul cage_

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Drain Soul Cage](/Feats.aspx?ID=3514) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 50

**Archetype** [Lich](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=179)

**Prerequisites**
[Lich Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3513); [Drain Bonded Item](/Actions.aspx?ID=13), spellbook

</column>

---

You make your [_soul cage_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545) into your bonded item, allowing you to harness its abundant magical energy. Once per day, when you use Drain Bonded Item, you can choose any spell in your spellbook that you can cast, even if you haven't prepared or cast it that day. Your _soul cage_ doesn't need to be on your person for you to use Drain Bonded Item. If your _soul cage_ is broken or destroyed, you can't use Drain Soul Cage. Even if you can Drain Bonded Item more than once per day, for instance if you are a universalist wizard, you can still only choose a spell you haven't prepared and cast once per day.
name: Drain Soul Cage

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Enshroud Soul Cage](/Feats.aspx?ID=3515) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 50

**Archetype** [Lich](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=179)

**Prerequisites**
[Lich Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3513)

</column>

---

You wreathe your [_soul cage_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545) in an ever-growing collection of obfuscating illusions and protective abjurations to hide it from your enemies. You disguise the _soul cage_ as any non-magical object of the  same Bulk, and it gains the effects of the [_magic aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=178) and [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) spells. The effects have unlimited durations, are heightened to half your level rounded up, and use your spell DC against any attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) them. The magic aura makes your _soul cage_ appear non-magical. You can ignore the effects of any of these spells when dealing with your own _soul cage_. You are mentally alerted whenever your _soul cage_ is damaged.
name: Enshroud Soul Cage

</column>

---

Negative energy intensifies your undead form and makes your very touch the antithesis of life. You gain the [advanced undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1695). The damage die for your fist increases to 1d6 instead of 1d4, it deals negative damage instead of bludgeoning damage, and it loses the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait. Your fist becomes [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103).

 When you critically hit a living creature with your fist Strike, the creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the end of your next turn unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save against your spell DC. This is a [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).
name: Hand of the Lich

    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bolster Soul Cage](/Feats.aspx?ID=3517) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Lich Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3513); Legendary in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

Your constant efforts to augment and perfect your [_soul cage_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545) have bolstered its efficiency. Your _soul cage_ has Hardness equal to your level and HP equal to four times your level. The time it takes your _soul cage_ to rebuild your body is reduced to 1d6 days rather than 1d10 days.

 At 20th level, the time it takes your _soul cage_ to rebuild your body is reduced to 2d12+12 hours.
name: Bolster Soul Cage

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spell Gem](/Feats.aspx?ID=3518) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 51

**Archetype** [Lich](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=179)

**Prerequisites**
[Lich Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3513); Legendary in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

Inspired by the degenerate [demiliches](/Monsters.aspx?ID=283) your kind sometimes devolves into, you've embedded a gem into your brow, fusing it to your necrotic flesh. This gem has been specially calibrated to hold one of the following spells: [_repulsion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=254), [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=268), or [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344). You must decide which spell your gem contains when you take this feat; changing the spell requires retraining the feat. Once per day, you can drain the gem's magical energy to Cast the Spell without spending a spell slot.

 At 18th level, you can heighten whichever spell you chose to 7th level. At 20th level, you can heighten whichever spell you chose to 8th level; regardless of which spell you chose, you can instead drain the gem's magical energy to cast an 8th-level [_spell turning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=297) without spending a spell slot.
name: Spell Gem

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mummy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3520) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 52

**Archetype** [Mummy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=180)

**Prerequisites**
you are dead and were mummified (by natural or ritualistic means)

</column>

---

Whether through natural processes or foul rituals, your body was mummified, and you've risen as an undead mummy. You gain the [mummy](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=74) and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) traits, as well as [basic undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1694).

 Many effects that harm the living are far less effective against your desiccated form. Unfortunately, your dried-out body is also highly flammable. You gain the [Toughness](/Feats.aspx?ID=855) feat, but also [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) weakness equal to half your level.

 Your fist draws moisture from the living by touch, making it more damaging and deadly. The damage die for your fist increases to 1d6 instead of 1d4, and your fist loses the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait.

 Finally, you become supernaturally bound to the dominant terrain in which you were created, either because you formed naturally from the weather of that terrain or because of the local ingredients and methods used in an intentional mummification process. Choose arctic, desert, mountain, or swamp. This choice can't be changed and may alter the effects of some of your feats.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the mummy archetype.

<title level="2">Mummy Dedication leads to...</title>
[Accursed Touch](/Feats.aspx?ID=3525), [Desiccating Inhalation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3530), [Ensnaring Wrappings](/Feats.aspx?ID=3528), [Grave Mummification](/Feats.aspx?ID=3523), [Mummy's Despair](/Feats.aspx?ID=3524), [One with the Land](/Feats.aspx?ID=3521), [Semblance of Life](/Feats.aspx?ID=3522), [Storm Shroud](/Feats.aspx?ID=3526), [Terrain Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=3529)
name: Mummy Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: you are dead and were mummified (by natural or ritualistic means)

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 52 **Archetype** [Mummy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=180) **Prerequisites** [Mummy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3520) --- Your connection to your bound terrain strengthens, making you unhindered by local weather. When in your bound terrain, you ignore the effects of non-magical difficult terrain. In addition, your experience from your bound terrain lets you ignore circumstance penalties to visual Perception checks and ignore [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) caused by certain environmental effects. The types you ignore depend on your bound terrain.

**Arctic** Ice or snow
**Desert** Dust or sand
**Mountain** Clouds or mist
**Swamp** Rain or murky water
name: One with the Land

Exposure to the elements, magic, alchemy, and other phenomena intensified the changes from your mummification. You gain the [advanced undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1695).

 In addition, you gain resistance equal to half your level against any damage you'd take from your bound terrain, including its [dangerous temperatures, weather, hazardous terrain, and environmental hazards](/Rules.aspx?ID=642).

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 52

**Archetype** [Mummy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=180)

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Mummy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3520)

</column>

---

You force your mental anguish outward, projecting it upon those around you. You gain an aura of despair in a 30-foot emanation lasting 5 rounds. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the aura must succeed at a Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC or be [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds at the save is temporarily immune to Mummy's Despair for 10 minutes.

<title level="2">Mummy's Despair leads to...</title>
[Greater Despair](/Feats.aspx?ID=3531)
name: Mummy's Despair

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Accursed Touch](/Feats.aspx?ID=3525) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Mummy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3520)

</column>

---

You've learned to harness the supernatural curse that animates you, imbuing your most powerful attacks with a terrible curse. When you critically hit with a fist Strike, negative energy binds to it; this is a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38) and a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46). The creature takes 2d6 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). While the creature has this persistent damage, it's also [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). The persistent damage increases to 3d6 at 14th level and 4d6 at 20th level. This is a [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<title level="2">Accursed Touch leads to...</title>
[Channel Rot](/Feats.aspx?ID=3527)
name: Accursed Touch

[Mummy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3520)

</column>

You become surrounded by a swirling storm of rain, sand, snow, or clouds, whichever of those suits your bound terrain. This activity has traits appropriate to the storm you create— typically [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) for sand and [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) for rain, snow, or clouds. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) for 1 minute. You can't use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), as normal for concealment that makes your position obvious.
name: Storm Shroud

</title>

<traits>

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Mummy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3520)

</column>

---

You draw in the moisture from nearby creatures, draining them dry to heal your wounds. Creatures in a 30-foot cone take 6d8 negative damage, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC. A creature that critically fails this saving throw is also [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). As long as at least one creature was damaged by your Desiccating Inhalation, you regain HP equal to your level.

 At 14th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d8.
name: Desiccating Inhalation

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[As in Life, So in Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=3532) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Skeleton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=236" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 56

</column>

---

Specific memories of your old life are hard to hold onto, but you know things without remembering why. You gain the [Adopted Ancestry](/Feats.aspx?ID=751) feat. You can choose any ancestry, but it is likely to be one that matches both your skeleton heritage and who you were in life. As long as your body is completely covered by armor or clothing, you do not have to attempt [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks against a creature's Perception DC to successfully [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) yourself as a member of that ancestry. This is a non-magical disguise that doesn't protect against divination. This disguise doesn't provide any benefit against a creature actively attempting a Perception check against you.
name: As in Life, So in Death

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 56

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Trigger**
An enemy's Strike against you is a critical hit.

</column>

---

You collapse into a pile of bones, mitigating the worst of the damage you would have taken, and the triggering attack deals only the amount it would deal on a hit (typically full damage instead of double damage). Any other effects caused by a critical hit still occur. While collapsed, you're [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) and can't act except to Stand, which re-forms you into a skeleton in a standing position.
name: Collapse

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Skeleton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=236" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 56

</column>

---

You lie [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and pretend to be an ordinary skeleton. As long as you don't move or take any actions requiring anything other than your mind, you appear dead. To see through your ruse, another creature must succeed at a check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC, either by [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or [Recalling Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) (typically using [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) or an appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill). This ability doesn't work against creatures that already know you're an animate undead unless you have changed your appearance.
name: Play Dead

You can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) undead or make simple requests of them. As long as there aren't living creatures nearby, they usually let you speak. If the undead creature currently is in the thrall of a creature whose level is higher than yours, you typically need a critical success on your Diplomacy skill check.
name: Undead Empathy

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Well-armed](/Feats.aspx?ID=3537) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Skeleton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=236" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 56

</column>

---

Your detachable limbs offer flexibility. You Interact to remove your arm and wield it in the other one, increasing your [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) by 5 feet for any one-handed weapon held in that arm. If your next action is a Strike with that weapon, creatures that were outside your reach that you can now hit are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against your first attack. You don't have a free hand while holding the arm. You can Interact to reattach the arm while holding it.
name: Well-armed

    level="1"
    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bone Missile](/Feats.aspx?ID=3538) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Skeleton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=236" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 56

</column>

---

You can remove your ribs to use them as arrows or bolts. When you draw a rib, you lose 2 HP, and the projectile deals 2 extra negative damage if you Strike with it before the end of your next turn. The HP loss and extra damage both increase to 3 at 12th level and 4 at 19th level. Your rib cage magically replenishes, and any rib you draw crumbles to dust after being used for a Strike or at the end of your next turn, whichever comes first.
name: Bone Missile

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rejuvenation Token](/Feats.aspx?ID=3539) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Skeleton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=236" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 56

</column>

---

You tie yourself to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) by implanting a piece of your consciousness into a weapon, piece of armor, or other item that's precious to you. As long as this object is intact, even if your body is destroyed, you can be restored to the exact state you were in the last time you made daily preparations with this object in your possession. The soulbound object can be used as a special focus for [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243), [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20), or similar magic. This warps the spell to bring you back in your skeletal undead state rather than bringing you back to life. [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) can't prevent you from returning when the spell is modified in this way.

 When you return, you permanently lose a memory that was precious to you, in addition to any other side effects of the spell that brought you back. Your soulbound object becomes inert until you spend 1 week of downtime to anchor yourself to it again, further fragmenting your mind.
name: Rejuvenation Token

You rearrange your bones into a bestial shape. Once per day, you can cast [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) as a divine innate spell. When you cast it, you choose the spell's level: 3rd, 4th, or 5th. Your battle form looks like a skeletal version of the animal. In this form, you retain the benefits of any skeleton feats that do not require the use of hands or the ability to communicate.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skeleton Commander](/Feats.aspx?ID=3542) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Skeleton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=236" />

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

You gain the ability to summon your own skeletal minions. You learn the [_create undead_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) rituals to create three types of undead for which you meet the prerequisites. These undead must have the [skeleton](/Traits.aspx?ID=236) trait or be other skeletal undead the GM agrees to. You can't teach these rituals to anyone else, and you can't participate in either ritual while allowing someone else to serve as the primary caster unless they also know the ritual.

 You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Religion checks for _create undead_ rituals. You can also perform the ritual without the aid of a secondary caster; in this case, you attempt the secondary check normally performed by that caster.
name: Skeleton Commander

markdown:
**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 57 **Frequency** once per day --- You scatter your body and transform into a whirling storm of bones for up to 1 minute or until you spend a single action to return to your normal shape. You become Huge, gain the [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=239) trait, and gain a fly Speed of 40 feet. As a swarm, you have the following characteristics.
  • You are immune to the [grappled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) conditions.
  • You have weakness 5 to area and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage.
  • You can occupy the same space as other creatures and must do so to use your damaging ability.
  • As a 2-action activity, you can deal 10d6 bludgeoning damage to all creatures sharing your space ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save with a DC equal to your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher).
  • You can't speak, Cast Spells, use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions requiring your hands, Activate magic items, or make any Strikes with your normal body.
  • You don't gain the [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36) ability, so you are still affected normally by [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. You also retain the benefits of skeleton feats that don't require communication or the use of hands.
name: Bone Swarm

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vampire Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3545) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 58

**Archetype** [Vampire](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=181)

**Prerequisites**
You were a killed by a vampire drinking your blood.

</column>

---

You're a newly risen vampire. You gain the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and [vampire](/Traits.aspx?ID=242) traits and the [basic undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1694), and your undead hunger is for the blood of the living. Your incisors elongate; you gain a fangs [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage. They're in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits. You gain revulsion and sunlight vulnerabilities detailed in the Vampire Vulnerabilities sidebar and the Drink Blood ability.

 **Drink Blood** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within your reach; **Effect** You sink your fangs into that creature and drink its blood. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed, and automatically succeeds for any of the other conditions. If you succeed, the creature becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), and you gain temporary HP equal to the target's level that last for 10 minutes. Further uses against the target don't increase the drained condition or grant you more temporary HP.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the vampire archetype.

<title level="2">Vampire Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bat Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=3552), [Clinging Climber](/Feats.aspx?ID=3546), [Coffin Bound](/Feats.aspx?ID=3554), [Daywalker](/Feats.aspx?ID=3549), [Manipulative Charm](/Feats.aspx?ID=3547), [Nocturnal Kindred](/Feats.aspx?ID=3548), [Predatory Claws](/Feats.aspx?ID=3550), [Turn to Mist](/Feats.aspx?ID=3553), [Vicious Fangs](/Feats.aspx?ID=3551)
name: Vampire Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: You were a killed by a vampire drinking your blood.

</title>

<traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Vampire Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3545); Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)

</column>

---

Against [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47), and to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) and [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50).

 Once per day, you can cast [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) as a divine innate spell using your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait. At 5th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the spell is heightened by an additional level, to a maximum of a 9th-level charm when you are 19th level.

<title level="2">Manipulative Charm leads to...</title>
[Dominating Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=3556)

You have an innate connection with other creatures of the night and understand some of them. You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) with bats, rats, and wolves. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on such animals.

 Once per day, you can cast [_animal allies_](/Spells.aspx?ID=863) as a divine innate spell, using your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. The creatures it summons are bats, rats, and wolves. At 5th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the spell is heightened by an additional level, to a maximum of a 9th-level _animal allies_ when you are 19th level.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Coffin Bound](/Feats.aspx?ID=3554) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Vampire](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=181)

**Prerequisites**
[Vampire Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3545)

</column>

---

You bind your spirit to a coffin filled with soil of your homeland, making it a place of recovery. If you rest inside your coffin for 10 minutes, you regain Hit Points equal to your Constitution modifier × half your level. You can still be healed in other ways during that time, though you're [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) and therefore unable to heal yourself. The GM might allow you to [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) while resting, depending on whether it's a type of Refocusing you can achieve while you slumber.

 If you would be destroyed, you instead remain near death. This doesn't change your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) or [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) value, and you remain unconscious. After 1 hour, you're destroyed. If returned to your coffin before that hour is up, you avoid destruction; if you spend 1 hour resting in your coffin, you regain 1 HP and lose the dying and wounded conditions. You can't be healed in any other way when near death.

 Your coffin can't heal you if you're staked through the chest. You can be staked only if unconscious, and it takes three actions. You lose all benefits of this feat if your coffin is destroyed or the soil is lost. You must procure a new coffin and fill it with soil of your homeland to restore the feat's function.

<title level="2">Coffin Bound leads to...</title>
[Mist Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=3555)
name: Coffin Bound
