markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mist Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=3555) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Vampire](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=181)

**Prerequisites**
[Turn to Mist](/Feats.aspx?ID=3553); [Coffin Bound](/Feats.aspx?ID=3554)

**Trigger**
You are reduced to 0 HP.

</column>

---

As you fall, your body reacts to defend itself. You Turn to Mist. This doesn't expend a use of Turn to Mist, and you can use Mist Escape even if you've already Turned to Mist in the same day. You take [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions to move directly toward your coffin even though you are at 0 HP, though you aren't conscious and don't have control over how you do so. While at 0 HP in this form, you are unaffected by further damage. You automatically return to your corporeal form, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), if you reach your coffin or after 1 hour when you are destroyed, whichever one comes first.
name: Mist Escape

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dominating Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=3556) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Vampire](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=181)

**Prerequisites**
[Manipulative Charm](/Feats.aspx?ID=3547)

</column>

---

You stare into your target's eyes and enthrall them, shattering their free will. Once per day, you can cast 7th-level [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) as a divine innate spell, using your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. It gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait. At 18th level, the dominate is heightened to 8th level; at 20th level, it's heightened to 9th level. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to your domination for 24 hours, though in most cases, you can't Cast the Spell again within 24 hours. If you are destroyed, all your _dominate_ spells from Dominating Gaze immediately end.
name: Dominating Gaze

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Zombie Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3557) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 60

**Archetype** [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182)

**Prerequisites**
You died and were animated as a [husk zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1919).

</column>

---

Despite your death and subsequent reanimation as a rotting corpse, you retain most of your mind and identity. Yet, it's always there: the hunger for the flesh and organs of the living. You gain the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and [zombie](/Traits.aspx?ID=245) traits, and the [basic undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1694). Unlike a typical zombie, you're not mindless. Reduce all your Speeds by 5 feet. Your fist loses the nonlethal trait.

 Because of its progressing state of decay, your body requires continual maintenance to remain at its best. Each day after your rest period, your body is deteriorated due to natural decay. Additionally, some zombie abilities allow you to push for a greater effect in exchange for a chance of deteriorating your body.

 While your body is deteriorated, you're [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). You can spend 10 minutes checking and repairing your joints and other weak parts of your body to recover from deterioration. You can perform these repairs as part of your daily preparations. Because it's a natural result of your body's functions, you can't remove the slowed condition from deterioration through any other means.

 You crave the flesh of the living. You gain a jaws unarmed attack that deals 1d8 piercing damage. Your jaws are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) trait. You can use your jaws Strike only against a creature that's [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) by you.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the zombie archetype.

<title level="2">Zombie Dedication leads to...</title>
[Ankle Biter](/Feats.aspx?ID=3558), [Feast](/Feats.aspx?ID=3559), [Festering Wound](/Feats.aspx?ID=3566), [Numb](/Feats.aspx?ID=3562), [Out of Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=3563), [Ravenous Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=3560), [Seize](/Feats.aspx?ID=3567), [Shamble](/Feats.aspx?ID=3564), [Unkillable](/Feats.aspx?ID=3565)
name: Zombie Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: You died and were animated as a [husk zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1919).

You fight just as well on the ground as you do standing up. While you are [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), you are always [Taking Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90) against ranged attacks, you ignore the status penalty on your attack rolls from being prone, and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40).

  You can choose to move up to half your Speed when you Crawl. You can instead move up to your full Speed when you Crawl, but if you do, you must succeed at a DC 6 flat check or your body deteriorates.
name: Ankle Biter

[Feast](/Feats.aspx?ID=3559) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />

**Requirements**
You are adjacent to a [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) creature, or a deceased creature that died in the past hour.

</column>

---

You feast upon an adjacent restrained or unconscious creature, or a deceased creature that died in the past hour. If the creature is alive, you deal damage equal to your jaws damage. Whether it's alive or not, you gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to half your level that last for 1 minute.

 You can instead choose to gain temporary Hit Points equal to double your level, but if you do, you must succeed at a DC 6 flat check or your body deteriorates.

<title level="2">Feast leads to...</title>
[Brains!](/Feats.aspx?ID=3561)
name: Feast

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Brains!](/Feats.aspx?ID=3561) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182)

**Prerequisites**
[Feast](/Feats.aspx?ID=3559)

**Trigger**
You Feast upon a [humanoid's](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) brain. The humanoid can't be [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) (or, if you're Feasting on a corpse, can't have been mindless while alive).

</column>

---

You consume the brains of your foe, causing synapses long rotten to fire and enabling you to shake off your mental torpor. You gain a +1 status bonus on skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) for 1 hour. If the humanoid had a higher modifier to the skill check you're using to Recall Knowledge than you do, the status bonus increases to +2.
name: Brains!

>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Zombie Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3557)

</column>

---

As your body continues to rot and putrefy, your senses deaden and your undead powers grow. You gain the [advanced undead benefits](/Rules.aspx?ID=1695), as well as a +1 circumstance bonus on saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and pain effects. While your body is deteriorated, your numbness amplifies in two ways: the circumstance bonus from this feat increases to +2, and if you roll a success on a saving throw against an emotion or pain effect, you get a critical success instead.
name: Numb

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Out of Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=3563) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Zombie](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=182)

**Prerequisites**
[Zombie Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3557)

</column>

---

Even when your limbs are severed, they remain a part of you. When an arm is severed from your body, it gains the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait. While severed, your limb has the statistics of a [familiar](/Rules.aspx?ID=160) without any familiar or master abilities, except its Speed is 5 feet, it can't move further than 100 feet from you, it can Interact with things, and it can still make any [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strikes it could have made while attached to you (usually a fist Strike). Any Strikes it makes share your multiple attack penalty and use your attack bonus and damage. If your arm is reduced to 0 HP or is ever more than 100 feet from you, it becomes inert until reattached. The GM might allow you to detach other limbs that have unarmed attacks using the below action (for example, if you're a tiefling with [Form of the Fiend](/Feats.aspx?ID=1379), giving you a tail unarmed attack).

 You gain the Lay Down Arms action.

 **Lay Down Arms** <actions string="Single Action" /> You pull your arm off, harmlessly severing it from your body and dropping it in an adjacent square. You can also use this action to reattach your severed arm if it's adjacent to you; it immediately functions normally. If the detached limb was at 0 Hit Points, it takes 10 minutes to reattach it instead of a single action.
name: Out of Hand
